# Kraftinox

> Industrial installation company specializing in orbital welding, pressure systems, and stainless steel piping for food, pharmaceutical and industrial sectors. Târgu Mureș, Romania. Founded 2011.

Kraftinox SRL executes precision industrial installations with full documentation and traceable execution. Certified by ISCIR and TÜV-Süd for pressure equipment (PED). Team of 20 specialists — IWT welders, fitters, thermal insulators. Own 250 m² workshop with orbital welding system, Olympus Iplex videoscope, Kemppi Master TIG and Esab equipment.

## Pages

- [Homepage RO](https://kraftinox.com/ro): Main site in Romanian — services, projects, about, certifications, contact
- [Homepage EN](https://kraftinox.com/en): Main site in English
- [Terms & Conditions RO](https://kraftinox.com/ro/termeni-si-conditii): GDPR privacy policy and terms of use
- [Terms & Conditions EN](https://kraftinox.com/en/termeni-si-conditii): GDPR privacy policy and terms of use

## Services

- Thermomechanical installations: heating, cooling and steam systems for industrial applications
- Technological process installations: food, pharmaceutical and industrial process systems
- Pressure systems: steam, compressed air, technical gases and high-pressure water (ISCIR certified)
- Orbital & TIG welding: stainless steel pipes and fittings to international standards
- Fire protection systems: hydrants and sprinklers per safety regulations
- Thermal insulation & cladding (jacketing): energy efficiency solutions
- Technical gas installations: oxygen, hydrogen, argon, CO2 and other industrial gases
- CIP systems: industrial clean-in-place systems for hygienic applications

## Certifications & Standards

- ISCIR (Romanian pressure equipment authority)
- TÜV-Süd — Pressure Equipment Directive (PED)
- IWT-qualified welders
- Full weld traceability: isometric maps, WPS, material specifications, test reports

## Partners (selection)

Richter Gedeon, Sandoz, Linde Gas, Bergenbier, Hochland, Knauf Insulation, Medex, Hilton, Messer, Uniprest, Evora, Sanlacta
